details: Beginning Phase 1 analysis of pack_tile usage patterns for eltwise_chain integration design
Bucket histogram: 14 heavy_lifting categories × flow patterns. Dominant: eltwise-fpu-binary(148), eltwise-fpu-bcast(111), copy-sfpu-tile(169), copy(76)
Sync style breakdown by category: Modern(526,79%), raw-dst(35,5%), ACQ-REL-macro(39,6%). Key finding: transformer ops need raw-dst support; normalization needs both modern and ACQ-REL-macro.
Loop shapes: modern-canonical/single(359,54%), single-in-loop(151,23%), multi-unrolled(33,5%), raw-dst/single(35,5%), ACQ-REL-macro variants(43,6%). Representative files identified for each.
Multi-pack per window: 209/666 rows(31%). Top patterns: CWPRACWPR(55), ACWPRACWPR(26), CWPRACWPRA(24). Evidence for block packing or composable multi-pack chaining.
Variable DEST index: 126/666 rows(19%). Loop vars(98): i,j,k. Fixed slots(28): dst_reg_0..3. Pattern: eltwise-binary(38), eltwise-bcast(22), sfpu-tile(18). MUST support indexed DEST.
Multi-CB routing: 101/157 files(64%) pack to 2-12 CBs. Top: moreh_layer_norm(12), moreh_layer_norm_backward(12), sdpa(9). CB must be runtime parameter, not static binding.

7.1: Modern[ACWR] required(79%), raw-dst should(5%), ACQ-REL-macro optional(6%). 7.2: Support block-pack or composable chaining. 7.3: Indexed DEST required. 7.4: CB parameter required. 7.5: Expose primitives, no forced loop policy.
Go/No-Go: YES, implement PackTile as chain element. Phase 1: modern[ACWR] single-pack (54% baseline). Phase 2: raw-dst + multi-pack. Phase 3+: ACQ-REL-macro migration, optimizations.

eltwise_v2_pack_analysis.md written to /localdev/astancov/tt-metal/ttnn/cpp/ttnn/kernel_lib/agents/. Sections: executive summary, bucket histogram, sync styles, loop shapes, multi-pack, variable DEST, multi-CB, design implications, recommendations, conclusion.
